messages.content: The simplest, fastest repository for training/finetuning medium-sized GPTs. It is a rewrite of minGPT that prioritizes teeth over education. Still under active development, but currently the file train.py reproduces GPT-2 (124M) on OpenWebText, running on a single 8XA100 40GB node in about 4 days of training. The code itself is plain and readable: train.py is a ~300-line boilerplate training loop and model.py a ~300-line GPT model definition, which can optionally load the GPT-2 weights from OpenAI. That's it.  翻译成中文

最简单、最快速的中等规模GPT（泛用预训练模型）训练/微调存储库。它是minGPT的重写版本，注重效率而非教育。目前仍在积极开发中，但目前的train.py文件可以在OpenWebText上复现GPT-2（124M）的训练，在单个8XA100 40GB节点上大约需要4天时间。代码本身简洁易读：train.py是一个大约300行的模板训练循环，model.py是一个大约300行的GPT模型定义，可以选择性地从OpenAI加载GPT-2的权重。就是这样。

Because the code is so simple, it is very easy to hack to your needs, train new models from scratch, or finetune pretrained checkpoints (e.g. biggest one currently available as a starting point would be the GPT-2 1.3B model from OpenAI).  翻译成中文
由于代码非常简单，因此非常容易根据自己的需求进行修改，从头开始训练新模型，或对预训练的检查点进行微调（例如，OpenAI目前提供的最大模型为GPT-2 1.3B模型，可以作为起点）。

If you are not a deep learning professional and you just want to feel the magic and get your feet wet, the fastest way to get started is to train a character-level GPT on the works of Shakespeare. First, we download it as a single (1MB) file and turn it from raw text into one large stream of integers:  翻译成中文
如果您不是深度学习专业人士，只是想感受一下魔力并入门，最快的方法是在莎士比亚的作品上训练一个字符级别的GPT。首先，我们将其以单个（1MB）文件的形式下载下来，并将其从原始文本转换为一个大的整数流：
This creates a train.bin and val.bin in that data directory. Now it is time to train your GPT. The size of it very much depends on the computational resources of your system:  翻译成中文
这将在数据目录下创建一个train.bin和一个val.bin文件。现在是训练GPT的时候了。其大小非常取决于您系统的计算资源：

If you peek inside it, you'll see that we're training a GPT with a context size of up to 256 characters, 384 feature channels, and it is a 6-layer Transformer with 6 heads in each layer. On one A100 GPU this training run takes about 3 minutes and the best validation loss is 1.4697. Based on the configuration, the model checkpoints are being written into the --out_dir directory out-shakespeare-char. So once the training finishes we can sample from the best model by pointing the sampling script at this directory:  翻译成中文

如果您查看其中的内容，您将会看到我们正在训练一个GPT模型，它的上下文大小可以达到256个字符，具有384个特征通道，是一个6层的Transformer模型，每层有6个头。在一个A100 GPU上，这个训练过程大约需要3分钟，最好的验证损失为1.4697。根据配置，模型检查点被写入到--out_dir参数指定的目录out-shakespeare-char中。因此，一旦训练完成，我们可以通过将采样脚本指向该目录来从最佳模型中进行采样：

lol ¯\_(ツ)_/¯. Not bad for a character-level model after 3 minutes of training on a GPU. Better results are quite likely obtainable by instead finetuning a pretrained GPT-2 model on this dataset (see finetuning section later).

I only have a macbook (or other cheap computer). No worries, we can still train a GPT but we want to dial things down a notch. I recommend getting the bleeding edge PyTorch nightly (select it here when installing) as it is currently quite likely to make your code more efficient. But even without it, a simple train run could look as follows:  翻译成中文

哈哈 ¯\_(ツ)_/¯。在GPU上训练3分钟后，对于一个字符级别的模型来说，效果还不错。通过在这个数据集上微调预训练的GPT-2模型，很有可能获得更好的结果（请参阅稍后的微调部分）。

我只有一台MacBook（或其他廉价计算机）。别担心，我们仍然可以训练一个GPT模型，但需要降低一些要求。我建议安装最新版本的PyTorch nightly版（在安装时选择它），因为目前它很可能会使您的代码更高效。但即使没有它，一个简单的训练运行可能如下所示：

Here, since we are running on CPU instead of GPU we must set both --device=cpu and also turn off PyTorch 2.0 compile with --compile=False. Then when we evaluate we get a bit more noisy but faster estimate (--eval_iters=20, down from 200), our context size is only 64 characters instead of 256, and the batch size only 12 examples per iteration, not 64. We'll also use a much smaller Transformer (4 layers, 4 heads, 128 embedding size), and decrease the number of iterations to 2000 (and correspondingly usually decay the learning rate to around max_iters with --lr_decay_iters). Because our network is so small we also ease down on regularization (--dropout=0.0). This still runs in about ~3 minutes, but gets us a loss of only 1.88 and therefore also worse samples, but it's still good fun:  翻译成中文

在这里，由于我们在CPU上而不是GPU上运行，我们必须设置--device=cpu，并且还要关闭PyTorch 2.0的编译，使用--compile=False。然后，当我们进行评估时，我们会得到更加嘈杂但更快的估计值（--eval_iters=20，而不是200），我们的上下文大小仅为64个字符而不是256个字符，每次迭代只有12个示例的批量大小，而不是64个。我们还将使用一个更小的Transformer模型（4层，4个头，128个嵌入维度），并将迭代次数减少到2000次（并相应地使用--lr_decay_iters来衰减学习率到最大迭代次数附近）。由于我们的网络非常小，我们也会减少正则化（--dropout=0.0）。这个训练仍然需要约3分钟，但损失只有1.88，因此样本也更差，但仍然很有趣：

Not bad for ~3 minutes on a CPU, for a hint of the right character gestalt. If you're willing to wait longer, feel free to tune the hyperparameters, increase the size of the network, the context length (--block_size), the length of training, etc.

Finally, on Apple Silicon Macbooks and with a recent PyTorch version make sure to add --device=mps (short for "Metal Performance Shaders"); PyTorch then uses the on-chip GPU that can significantly accelerate training (2-3X) and allow you to use larger networks. See Issue 28 for more.

reproducing GPT-2
A more serious deep learning professional may be more interested in reproducing GPT-2 results. So here we go - we first tokenize the dataset, in this case the OpenWebText, an open reproduction of OpenAI's (private) WebText:  翻译成中文

对于CPU运行而言，大约3分钟的时间得到的效果还不错，可以提供一些正确字符组合的线索。如果你愿意等待更长的时间，随意调整超参数、增加网络的大小、上下文长度（--block_size）、训练长度等等。

最后，在Apple Silicon的MacBook上，并且使用了最新版本的PyTorch，请确保添加--device=mps（即“Metal Performance Shaders”）；PyTorch将使用芯片上的GPU来加速训练（2-3倍），并允许您使用更大的网络。更多信息请参见Issue 28。

复现GPT-2
更专业的深度学习专家可能更感兴趣于复现GPT-2的结果。下面是具体的步骤-首先我们对数据集进行分词处理，例如OpenWebText，这是对OpenAI的（私有）WebText进行开放式复现的数据集：

This downloads and tokenizes the OpenWebText dataset. It will create a train.bin and val.bin which holds the GPT2 BPE token ids in one sequence, stored as raw uint16 bytes. Then we're ready to kick off training. To reproduce GPT-2 (124M) you'll want at least an 8X A100 40GB node and run:


This will run for about 4 days using PyTorch Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) and go down to loss of ~2.85. Now, a GPT-2 model just evaluated on OWT gets a val loss of about 3.11, but if you finetune it it will come down to ~2.85 territory (due to an apparent domain gap), making the two models ~match.

If you're in a cluster environment and you are blessed with multiple GPU nodes you can make GPU go brrrr e.g. across 2 nodes like:  翻译成中文

这个过程会下载并对OpenWebText数据集进行分词处理。它将创建一个train.bin和val.bin文件，其中存储了以原始uint16字节形式保存的GPT2 BPE标记的序列。然后我们准备开始训练。要复现GPT-2（124M），您至少需要一台8XA100 40GB节点，并运行以下命令：

这将使用PyTorch分布式数据并行（DDP）进行大约4天的训练，并将损失降低到约2.85左右。现在，仅在OWT上评估的GPT-2模型的验证损失约为3.11，但如果您对其进行微调，它将降至约2.85的范围（由于明显的领域差异），使得这两个模型基本匹配。

如果您在一个集群环境中且有多个GPU节点，您可以像下面这样让GPU全速运行，例如跨两个节点：

It is a good idea to benchmark your interconnect (e.g. iperf3). In particular, if you don't have Infiniband then also prepend NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1 to the above launches. Your multinode training will work, but most likely crawl. By default checkpoints are periodically written to the --out_dir. We can sample from the model by simply $ python sample.py.

Finally, to train on a single GPU simply run the $ python train.py script. Have a look at all of its args, the script tries to be very readable, hackable and transparent. You'll most likely want to tune a number of those variables depending on your needs.

baselines
OpenAI GPT-2 checkpoints allow us to get some baselines in place for openwebtext. We can get the numbers as follows:  翻译成中文

对于集群内部连接的基准测试是一个好主意（例如iperf3）。特别是，如果您没有Infiniband，还需要在上述启动命令之前添加NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1。多节点训练可能会运行，但可能会很慢。默认情况下，检查点会定期写入--out_dir目录中。我们可以通过简单地运行 $ python sample.py 从模型中进行采样。

最后，要在单个GPU上进行训练，只需运行 $ python train.py 脚本即可。查看所有参数，脚本试图非常易读、易于修改和透明。根据您的需求，您很可能需要调整其中许多变量。

基准测试
OpenAI GPT-2的检查点允许我们在openwebtext上建立一些基线。我们可以按如下方式获取这些数值：

However, we have to note that GPT-2 was trained on (closed, never released) WebText, while OpenWebText is just a best-effort open reproduction of this dataset. This means there is a dataset domain gap. Indeed, taking the GPT-2 (124M) checkpoint and finetuning on OWT directly for a while reaches loss down to ~2.85. This then becomes the more appropriate baseline w.r.t. reproduction.

finetuning
Finetuning is no different than training, we just make sure to initialize from a pretrained model and train with a smaller learning rate. For an example of how to finetune a GPT on new text go to data/shakespeare and run prepare.py to download the tiny shakespeare dataset and render it into a train.bin and val.bin, using the OpenAI BPE tokenizer from GPT-2. Unlike OpenWebText this will run in seconds. Finetuning can take very little time, e.g. on a single GPU just a few minutes. Run an example finetuning like:

$ python train.py config/finetune_shakespeare.py
This will load the config parameter overrides in config/finetune_shakespeare.py (I didn't tune them much though). Basically, we initialize from a GPT2 checkpoint with init_from and train as normal, except shorter and with a small learning rate. If you're running out of memory try decreasing the model size (they are {'gpt2', 'gpt2-medium', 'gpt2-large', 'gpt2-xl'}) or possibly decreasing the block_size (context length). The best checkpoint (lowest validation loss) will be in the out_dir directory, e.g. in out-shakespeare by default, per the config file. You can then run the code in sample.py --out_dir=out-shakespeare:  翻译成中文

然而，我们必须注意的是，GPT-2是在（封闭的、未发布的）WebText上进行训练的，而OpenWebText只是对这个数据集的最佳努力的开放复制。这意味着存在数据集领域差异。实际上，将GPT-2（124M）的检查点直接在OWT上进行微调，一段时间后的损失可以降低到约2.85。这就成为了与复现相关的更合适的基准。

微调
微调与训练没有区别，只需确保从预训练模型初始化，并使用较小的学习率进行训练。如果要了解如何在新文本上对GPT进行微调的示例，请转到data/shakespeare目录，并运行prepare.py下载微小的莎士比亚数据集，并使用来自GPT-2的OpenAI BPE分词器将其转换为train.bin和val.bin。与OpenWebText不同，这将在几秒钟内完成。微调可能只需要很少的时间，例如在单个GPU上只需几分钟。运行一个示例的微调命令，如下所示：

$ python train.py config/finetune_shakespeare.py
这将加载config/finetune_shakespeare.py中的配置参数覆盖（尽管我没有对它们进行太多调整）。基本上，我们从一个GPT2的检查点（使用init_from参数）进行初始化，并像正常训练一样进行训练，只是时间较短，使用较小的学习率。如果内存
